name: libnet-ssleay-perl
title: libnet-ssleay-perl
summary: Perl module for Secure Sockets Layer (SSL)
description:
Perl module for Secure Sockets Layer (SSL)
Net::SSLeay is a perl module that allows you to call Secure Sockets Layer
(SSL) functions of the SSLeay library directly from your perl scripts. It
is useful if you want to program robots that access secure web servers or
if you want to build your own applications over SSL encrypted tunnels. If
you just want to view web pages on https servers, you do not need this -
your web browser already knows to do that.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libnet-ssleay-perl because Perl module for Secure Sockets Layer (SSL).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libnet-ssleay-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libnet-ssleay-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libnet-ssleay-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
